question_text: A cyclist travels 45 km in 1.5 hours. Calculate her average speed in km/h, then convert it to m/s. Show your working.
explanation: First, use speed = distance ÷ time: 45 ÷ 1.5 = 30 km/h. To convert to m/s, divide by 3.6 (or convert: 30,000 m ÷ 3600 s = 8.33 m/s). This shows you can apply the formula and handle unit conversions.
